description: A wrapper that pins a value in memory, preventing it from being moved. Pin is essential for self-referential types and is used extensively with async/await and Futures. When a value is pinned, it is guaranteed to stay at the same memory address until it is dropped.

methods.name: new
methods.description: Creates a new Pin wrapping a pointer. Only works if the pointer's target implements Unpin (safe to move).

methods.name: as_ref
methods.description: Returns a Pin of a shared reference to the pinned value. Useful for passing pinned references without consuming the Pin.

methods.name: as_mut
methods.signature:
fn as_mut(&mut self) -> Pin<&mut

::Target>
methods.description: Returns a Pin of a mutable reference to the pinned value.

methods.name: get_ref
methods.description: Returns a shared reference to the pinned value. This is safe because a shared reference cannot move the value.

methods.name: into_inner
methods.description: Unwraps the Pin and returns the underlying pointer. Only available when the target type implements Unpin.
